The namespace field of the modules references returned by cps will have a null in them. This was done as part of CPS-506 and because of this the If statement here needs to be revisited. For example, with the current code the following would be added to missing modules list even though its not a missing modules.
Module reference for a module already in cps.
ModuleReference {
  name:"someModule",
  nameSpace: null,
  revision: "someRevision"
}
From DMI we get this moduleReference.
ModuleReference {
  name:"someModule",
  nameSpace: "someNamespace",
  revision: "someRevision"
}
The current code will say that this is a new module and add it to the missing modules list even though it is not.
This should be the list of modules, module references for the modules that are in cps that the node requires. Right now you are passing in all the module references that the node requires.
I need more clarification on this as I think the port is not actually needed here. Internally I think it will resolve to 80?
As discussed this is correct. The response will send attribute "yang-source"

There is a spy here to avoid modelSync being called in objectUNderTest. If it si called then all the setup will need to be added for it to pass. objectUnderTest.modelSync(_) >> null will avoid calling this method
I added this as the list of missing modules was being reported as a useless object. After the refactoring on in modelSync this is no longer needed and has been removed.
